dependencies: access to original workbook to add Traceability sheet and export formulas
agreement on remediation multiplier cap and droplet QC policy from SME if needed
verification_steps_after_revision: Confirm assay_call.md contains normalization rationale and references the Traceability sheet.
Confirm calculations.txt matches formulas in workbook and reproduces per-record scores when executed against input_rows.csv.
Run deterministic checks: record_count=4, option_count=3, threshold and warn floor unchanged, and budget_feasible_combos unchanged.
Notify grader/verifier to re-run artifact-contract precheck and semantic review.

dependencies: access to original workbook to add Traceability sheet and export formulas
agreement on remediation multiplier cap and droplet QC policy from SME if needed
verification_steps_after_revision: Confirm assay_call.md contains normalization rationale and references the Traceability sheet.
Confirm calculations.txt matches formulas in workbook and reproduces per-record scores when executed against input_rows.csv.

Notify grader/verifier to re-run artifact-contract precheck and semantic review.

planned_changes: In assay_call.md: add a dedicated 'Normalization Rationale' subsection that (a) states the chosen denominator (embedded maximum), (b) provides justification (why max-to-100 is appropriate here), and (c) shows a one-paragraph alternative-normalization summary plus the already-calculated sensitivity impact (tie that to provided sensitivity table).

In both assay_call.md and the workbook: implement and document an explicit cap on remediation copy_ratio improvements (e.g., cap resulting copy_ratio improvement at 100% of baseline or 1.0 normalized), and add a short note describing how multiple combos are applied and how tie-breakers are resolved.

Introduce an explicit droplet QC policy: either declare an absolute droplet-count threshold used to gate calls or explain why relative droplet normalization is used; add a column in workbook and memo showing which records would be flagged under a chosen absolute threshold.

Add a new 'Traceability' worksheet that maps every key calculation output (score, call, margin_to_threshold, remediation results) to the exact source cells from 'Source_Packet' and show the formula text in a cell adjacent to each mapping.

Export and include in the repository a plain-text file (e.g., calculations.txt) containing the exact formulas used for scoring/classification/remediation, and include a small reproducible CSV (input_rows.csv) plus a short calculation script/pseudocode snippet in assay_call.md so reviewers can re-run calculations without openpyxl.
Clarify rounding/precision policy in assay_call.md and ensure the workbook uses the same rounding rules (document numeric precision used when reporting 'score' and 'max_score').
Regenerate ddpcr_copy_number.xlsx with the Traceability sheet and calculations.txt included, and update assay_call.md to reference the new files and the locations of mapped formulas.
raw_llm_output.revision_reason: Address minor reproducibility and assumption-clarity issues flagged by grader/verifier: normalization denominator justification, remediation multiplier cap, droplet QC threshold, and formula-level traceability for environments lacking xlsx parsing.

metadata_json.revision_goal: Baseline deliverables are present and well-structured (assay_call.md plus workbook). Graders and verifier concur that the packet documents scoring, classification thresholds, sensitivity checks, remediation combos, and explicitly flags ambiguities. Minor, actionable issues remain: (1) normalization denominator was assumed (embedded maximum) and needs justification or alternatives; (2) remediation copy_ratio improvement multiplier lacks an explicit cap and tie-breaker rules; (3) absolute droplet QC threshold is not specified; and (4) spreadsheet formula-level traceability is limited in environments where openpyxl is unavailable. Addressing these will improve reproducibility and reviewer confidence.
